subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Accumulation Function g(x) = ∫₂ˣ f(t)dt — Relative Extrema, Inflection, Abs Min/Max & Sign
stimulus: The figure shows the graph of the piecewise-linear function $f$. For $-4 \le x \le 12$, the function $g$ is defined by $g(x) = \int_2^x f(t)\, dt$.

parts.prompt: Does $g$ have a relative minimum, a relative maximum, or neither at $x = 10$? Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses $g'(x) = f(x)$ and checks sign of $f$ near $x = 10$
1 pt: Neither: $g'(x) = f(x) \le 0$ for $8 \le x \le 12$ (i.e., $f$ does not change sign at $x = 10$), so $g$ has neither a relative minimum nor relative maximum at $x = 10$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g'(x) = f(x) \le 0$ for $8 \le x \le 12$, so no sign change at $x = 10$. $g$ has neither a relative minimum nor maximum at $x = 10$.

parts.prompt: Does the graph of $g$ have a point of inflection at $x = 4$? Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses $g'(x) = f(x)$; checks whether $g''(x) = f'(x)$ changes sign at $x = 4$
1 pt: Yes, point of inflection at $x = 4$ because $g'(x) = f(x)$ is increasing for $2 \le x \le 4$ and decreasing for $4 \le x \le 8$ (i.e., $f'(x)$ changes sign from positive to negative at $x = 4$)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g'(x) = f(x)$ changes from increasing to decreasing at $x = 4$, so $g''$ changes sign at $x = 4$. The graph of $g$ has a point of inflection at $x = 4$.

parts.prompt: Find the absolute minimum value and the absolute maximum value of $g$ on the interval $-4 \le x \le 12$. Justify your answers.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Considers $x = -2$ and $x = 6$ as critical point candidates (where $f(x) = 0$ and $f$ changes sign)
1 pt: Evaluates $g$ at candidates using geometric areas: $g(-4)$, $g(-2)$, $g(6)$, $g(12)$; from scoring key: $g(-4) = -4$, $g(-2) = -8$, $g(6) = 8$, $g(12) = -4$
1 pt: Correct conclusion: absolute minimum value is $g(-2) = -8$ and absolute maximum value is $g(6) = 8$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g(-4) = -4$, $g(-2) = -8$, $g(6) = 8$, $g(12) = -4$ (computed via geometric areas of piecewise linear $f$). Absolute minimum is $g(-2) = -8$; absolute maximum is $g(6) = 8$.

parts.prompt: For $-4 \le x \le 12$, find all intervals for which $g(x) \le 0$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct intervals: $-4 \le x \le 2$ and $10 \le x \le 12$ (where $g(x) \le 0$)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g(x) \le 0$ for $-4 \le x \le 2$ and $10 \le x \le 12$.
